core_belief_tracker.title: Core Belief & Schema Monitoring System
core_belief_tracker.description: Long-term tracking of deep cognitive structures, their origins, manifestations, and transformation

belief_levels.core_beliefs: Deep, unconditional, global beliefs about self/others/world

defectiveness_shame.core_belief: I am inherently flawed, defective, and unlovable
defectiveness_shame.conditional_version: If people really knew me, they would reject me

transformation_targets.new_belief: I am worthy of love and belonging exactly as I am
intermediate_steps: I have flaws like everyone else
My imperfections don't make me unlovable
Some people will accept me as I am
I can be vulnerable and still be accepted
evidence_collection: List 5 people who care about me despite knowing my flaws
Track times when vulnerability led to connection
Record mistakes that didn't result in rejection

abandonment_instability.core_belief: I will inevitably be abandoned; relationships don't last
abandonment_instability.conditional_version: If I get too attached, I'll lose them
abandonment_instability.unconditional_version: I am destined to be alone

coping_modes.surrender: Accepting relationships are temporary, not investing
coping_modes.overcompensation: Excessive caretaking, inability to be alone

transformation_targets.new_belief: I can handle being alone and I can trust in lasting relationships
intermediate_steps: Some relationships do last
I can survive if someone leaves
My needs don't drive people away
I can tolerate uncertainty in relationships
evidence_collection: Track relationship durations that exceeded expectations
Practice small separations without catastrophe
Identify stable relationships in life

mistrust_abuse.core_belief: People will hurt, use, or take advantage of me
mistrust_abuse.conditional_version: If I trust someone, they will betray me
mistrust_abuse.unconditional_version: The world is dangerous and people are malicious

coping_modes.surrender: Accepting victim role, staying in abusive situations

transformation_targets.new_belief: I can protect myself and still have healthy connections with trustworthy people
intermediate_steps: Not everyone is dangerous
I can spot red flags now
Some people have proven trustworthy
Vulnerability doesn't guarantee harm
evidence_collection: List people who have been consistently trustworthy
Track times when trust was rewarded
Identify own improved boundary-setting abilities

dependence_incompetence.core_belief: I am incapable of handling life independently
dependence_incompetence.conditional_version: If I don't have help, I will fail
dependence_incompetence.unconditional_version: I am incompetent and need others to survive

transformation_targets.new_belief: I am capable of learning and handling life's challenges
intermediate_steps: I have succeeded at some things alone
I can learn what I don't know
Asking for help is different from dependence
Failure is part of learning, not proof of incompetence
evidence_collection: Log independent successes daily
Track new skills learned
Identify times when help wasn't needed

vulnerability_harm.core_belief: Catastrophe is always about to happen; I cannot cope
vulnerability_harm.conditional_version: If I'm not constantly vigilant, disaster will strike
vulnerability_harm.unconditional_version: The world is dangerous and I am vulnerable

coping_modes.surrender: Staying in safe bubble, limited living

transformation_targets.new_belief: I can handle challenges when they arise; I don't need to predict the future
intermediate_steps: Most worries never happen
I have survived past difficulties
Worry doesn't prevent bad things
I can cope with uncertainty
evidence_collection: Track worry predictions vs outcomes
List past crises survived
Record times when lack of worry didn't cause disaster

emotional_deprivation.core_belief: My emotional needs will never be met by others
emotional_deprivation.conditional_version: If I express needs, I will be disappointed or burdensome
emotional_deprivation.unconditional_version: I am destined to be emotionally alone

transformation_targets.new_belief: I deserve emotional nourishment and can ask for it
intermediate_steps: My needs are valid, not burdensome
Some people are capable of meeting needs
I can meet some of my own emotional needs
Asking doesn't guarantee disappointment
evidence_collection: Track times when needs were met when expressed
Identify safe people to practice with
Record self-nurturing activities

social_isolation.core_belief: I am fundamentally different and don't belong anywhere
social_isolation.conditional_version: If I try to fit in, people will see how different I am

coping_modes.overcompensation: Conformity at cost of authenticity, people-pleasing

transformation_targets.new_belief: I can find my people and belong while being authentically myself
intermediate_steps: Difference doesn't mean exclusion
There are communities for my specific interests/identity
I can connect over shared humanity
Authenticity attracts right people
evidence_collection: Identify times when you felt belonging
Find niche communities online or local
Track authentic connections made

failure.core_belief: I am incompetent and will fail at important life tasks
failure.conditional_version: If I try something important, I will fail

coping_modes.avoidance: Avoiding any situation where failure possible

transformation_targets.new_belief: I am capable of growth and can learn from setbacks
intermediate_steps: Failure is feedback, not identity
I have succeeded at some things
Effort matters more than outcome
Everyone fails; it's part of success
evidence_collection: Log all successes, especially small ones
Reframe past failures as learning
Track effort vs outcome separation

entitlement_grandiosity.core_belief: I am superior and deserving of special treatment
entitlement_grandiosity.conditional_version: If I don't get what I want, it's an injustice
entitlement_grandiosity.unconditional_version: Rules don't apply to me; I am special

coping_modes.avoidance: Cutting off anyone who doesn't comply

transformation_targets.new_belief: I am equal to others; my worth doesn't depend on being special
intermediate_steps: Everyone has equal inherent worth
Cooperation brings more than domination
Vulnerability creates real connection
Humility is strength, not weakness
evidence_collection: Track consequences of entitled behavior
Practice gratitude and appreciation
Notice others' contributions and worth

subjugation.core_belief: My needs are less important; I must yield to others
subjugation.conditional_version: If I express needs, I will be rejected or punished
subjugation.unconditional_version: I don't count; others come first

transformation_targets.new_belief: My needs matter equally; I can assert myself without guilt
intermediate_steps: My needs are valid, not selfish
Saying no doesn't make me bad
Healthy relationships allow for both people's needs
I can tolerate others' disappointment
evidence_collection: Track times when asserting needs didn't cause rejection
Identify relationships that survived your boundaries
Record self-care activities without guilt

self_sacrifice.core_belief: I must meet others' needs at my own expense to be good
self_sacrifice.conditional_version: If I prioritize myself, I am selfish and bad
self_sacrifice.unconditional_version: My worth comes from helping others
origin_themes: Parental message that needs are selfish

coping_modes.overcompensation: Selfish behavior with guilt, then overcompensating

transformation_targets.new_belief: I can care for others while also caring for myself
intermediate_steps: Self-care enables sustainable helping
I matter too
Healthy giving feels good, not depleting
I can receive as well as give
evidence_collection: Track resentment as indicator of overgiving
Record times when self-care improved helping capacity
Identify people who give to you

approval_seeking.core_belief: I must be approved of and admired to have worth
approval_seeking.conditional_version: If I'm not praised, I am worthless
approval_seeking.unconditional_version: My value depends on others' opinions
origin_themes: Parental narcissism using child for status

coping_modes.avoidance: Avoiding any situation where might not shine
coping_modes.overcompensation: Rejection of all approval, false indifference

transformation_targets.new_belief: I have inherent worth regardless of others' opinions
intermediate_steps: Approval is nice but not necessary
I can validate myself
Not everyone will like me, and that's okay
Authenticity brings deeper connection than performance
evidence_collection: Track internal vs external validation sources
Practice acting without seeking approval
Notice when criticism doesn't destroy you

negativity_pessimism.core_belief: Life is suffering; the worst will always happen
negativity_pessimism.conditional_version: If I expect the worst, I won't be disappointed
negativity_pessimism.unconditional_version: The world is bleak and hopeless
behavioral_manifestations: Not trying due to predicted failure

coping_modes.avoidance: Not engaging with life to avoid disappointment

transformation_targets.new_belief: Life contains both joy and pain; I can hope while being realistic
intermediate_steps: Pessimism hasn't protected me from pain
Good things do happen sometimes
Hope feels better than dread
I can handle disappointment if it comes
evidence_collection: Log positive events and good outcomes
Track accuracy of negative predictions
Practice hope in small ways

punitiveness.core_belief: People should be punished harshly for mistakes; forgiveness is weak
punitiveness.conditional_version: If I forgive, they will just hurt me again

transformation_targets.new_belief: People make mistakes; accountability can coexist with compassion
intermediate_steps: Punishment doesn't prevent future harm
Forgiveness is for me, not them
I have made mistakes too
Justice can include restoration
evidence_collection: Track cost of holding grudges on self
Notice when compassion leads to better outcomes
Practice self-forgiveness

unrelenting_standards.core_belief: I must always achieve at the highest standards; otherwise I am worthless
unrelenting_standards.conditional_version: If I relax, everything will fall apart
unrelenting_standards.unconditional_version: I must be perfect to be acceptable

coping_modes.avoidance: Procrastination due to fear of imperfect result
coping_modes.overcompensation: Complete giving up, rebellion against standards

transformation_targets.new_belief: I am worthy regardless of achievement; good enough is enough
intermediate_steps: Perfection is impossible and unnecessary
Rest improves performance
I can be loved without earning it
Done is better than perfect
evidence_collection: Track times when 'good enough' worked
Record consequences of overwork
Notice acceptance from others despite imperfection

core_belief_log.frequency: Daily during active work, weekly maintenance

structure.back: Evidence against, alternative belief, action plan

stage_1_awareness.markers: Can name beliefs and their triggers

title: Core Belief Tracker
description: Track activated core beliefs, evidence checks, and belief shift over time.

questions.question: Trigger event
questions.placeholder: What happened just before the belief activated?

questions.question: Activated core belief statement
questions.placeholder: Example: I am not good enough

questions.question: Evidence supporting this belief
questions.placeholder: Facts that seem to support it...

questions.question: Balanced alternative belief/thought
questions.placeholder: A more accurate and compassionate view...

questions.question: Behavioral experiment you will try
questions.placeholder: One small action to test the new belief

questions.question: Follow-up result or note
questions.placeholder: What happened after trying the action?
